name: Buche De Noel
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a jelly-roll pan with waxed paper and grease it lightly.
2. In a small bowl, sift together the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat the eggs until thick and creamy.
4. Gradually add sugar to the eggs, beating constantly until the mixture is very thick.
5. Stir in the cold brewed coffee and vanilla extract.
6. Fold in the flour mixture until just combined.
7. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
8. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 12 minutes or until set.
9. Sprinkle a kitchen towel with powdered sugar, invert the cake onto the towel, remove the waxed paper, and roll the cake tightly into the towel. Let cool completely.
10. When the cake has cooled, unroll it and spread with filling evenly.
11. Re-roll the cake to form one continuous roll about 3-1/2 inches wide.
12. In a large bowl, beat the heavy cream until stiff peaks form.
13. Add instant coffee and powdered sugar to the cream; mix until well combined.
14. Melt chocolate chips and coffee in a small saucepan over hot (not boiling) water. Let cool slightly.
15. In a separate bowl, beat butter until light and fluffy.
16. Beat corn syrup and egg yolks into the butter mixture.
17. Stir in the cooled chocolate mixture.
18. Frost the rolled cake as desired.
Special Note: This recipe is excellent for creating a vertical layer cake. After cooling, cut the cake into three 3-1/2-inch wide strips, fill them, and roll again to form one continuous 3-1/2-inch high cake before frosting. It appears like a standard two-layer cake until sliced.
